heading: Powers
label: 262 Powers
text: The Federal Advisory Council shall have power, by itself or through its officers, (1) to confer directly with the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System on general business conditions; (2) to make oral or written representations concerning matters within the jurisdiction of said board; (3) to call for information and to make recommendations in regard to discount rates, rediscount business, note issues, reserve conditions in the various districts, the purchase and sale of gold or securities by reserve banks, open-market operations by said banks, and the general affairs of the reserve banking system.
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of second par. of section 12 of act . First par. of section 12 is classified to .Dec. 23, 1913section 261 of this title
Change of NameSection 203(a) of act , changed name of Federal Reserve Board to Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.Aug. 23, 1935
